figures.caption: Fig. 4: Distribution of InHabitants by action type (left) and by interacted object category (right), showing broad coverage of both diverse activities and scene elements.

Fig. 3: Representative samples from InHabitants. Each pair shows the edited RGB image (left) and the corresponding lifted 3D human in the 3D scene (right). Our dataset contains diverse, context-aware interactions across varied indoor environments.

Fig. 5: Qualitative comparison. Our method generates diverse interactions, including those with rare objects (tiger, fireplace) and touch-less interactions (map). It also produces natural poses fitting for the context of the object (sofa, pool).

Fig. 2: Scalable 2D-to-3D human–scene interaction generation. Overview of InHabit. Left : affordance-aware 2D insertion—from rendered scene views, a VLM proposes context-consistent interactions and an image-editing model synthesizes humans in the image. Right : 3D scene-aware lifting—the inserted humans are recovered as SMPL-X meshes and optimized with scene geometry and proximity-based contact constraints for physically plausible 3D HSI.
